frameworks.name: range adaptation
frameworks.one_line: Neural sensitivity rescales inversely with the range of encoded values

frameworks.name: subjective economic value coding
frameworks.one_line: OFC neurons linearly encode subjective values during binary choice

frameworks.name: menu invariance
frameworks.one_line: Complementary property — value coding independent of alternative goods

core_question: Does the orbitofrontal value representation adapt to the range of values available in a given choice context?
key_assumptions: Choice patterns provide a behavioral measure of subjective value
OFC neuronal firing linearly encodes subjective value
Sensitivity is the change in activity per unit of value
manipulations: Varied value ranges across sessions in a juice-choice task
Measured single-neuron responses across different range conditions
Compared neuronal sensitivity and activity range across ranges
key_findings: OFC neurons encoded value linearly within each session
Neuronal sensitivity was inversely proportional to the value range
Neuronal activity range did not depend on the value range
Range adaptation complements menu invariance previously reported
interpretations: OFC value coding adapts to behavioral context by rescaling sensitivity
Each neuron adapts to its encoded range but ignores other available goods
